description:
The frontend (React 18, Vite, Vitest, ESLint) has full build/test tooling in
frontend/package.json (scripts: dev, build, lint, test) but .github/workflows/ci.yml
only runs Python jobs (make lint, pytest). The entire frontend surface is outside
the quality gate.

Add a Node CI job that runs:
- npm ci (or equivalent lockfile install)
- npm run lint (eslint)
- npm run build (tsc -b && vite build)
- npm run test (vitest run)

This should be a separate job in the existing ci.yml workflow, parallelized with
the Python jobs. Use Node 20 LTS. Cache node_modules for speed.

Acceptance criteria:
1. CI runs frontend lint, build, and test on every PR and push to main
2. Frontend failures block merge (same as Python failures)
3. Node modules are cached between runs
4. Job runs in parallel with existing Python jobs (not sequential)

Files:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (add Node job)
- frontend/package.json (existing scripts, no changes needed)
title: Add frontend lint/build/test job to CI workflow

description:
Introduce a 'staffer' agent type alongside the existing 'butler' type. Staffers are infrastructure agents (switchboard, messenger) that serve the ecosystem rather than the user directly. They share the same daemon engine but have different wiring: cross-butler permissions, exclusion from user-message routing, exclusion from daily briefing contributions, and infrastructure-contract manifestos instead of user-relationship manifestos.

Full spec: openspec/changes/add-staffer-concept/ (proposal.md, design.md, specs/, tasks.md)

Key design decisions:
- Single type field in butler.toml (butler|staffer), default butler
- Cross-butler permissions via [butler.permissions].cross_butler_access
- Routing exclusion at classification layer (staffers register but aren't routable for user messages)
- Infrastructure contract replaces manifesto framing for staffers
- Designed for N staffers (future qa_staffer without architecture changes)

Reclassifies switchboard and messenger from butler to staffer type.
title: Add staffer archetype to Butlers ecosystem

description:
Update the switchboard's message classifier and agent registry to exclude staffer-typed agents from user-message routing. Also update correct_route tool to reject re-dispatch targets that are staffers.

What changes:
- Switchboard classifier: filter candidate set to type=butler only during user-message classification
- Switchboard agent registry: store and expose the type field from registration payloads
- correct_route tool: validate target is butler-typed; return error if target is a staffer

close_reason: Gen-1 reconciliation complete: all 6 delta specs fully covered by sibling beads .1-.7. No gap beads needed. opsx:sync already run. Full checklist in worker report.
description:
Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (see epic description and openspec/changes/add-staffer-concept/) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Re-read all spec files in openspec/changes/add-staffer-concept/specs/ (staffer-archetype, butler-base-spec, butler-switchboard, butler-messenger, core-daemon, cross-butler-briefing-contribution).
3. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
4. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement and scenario to its implementing bead and code location.
5. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
6. If gap beads were created in step 5, create a follow-up reconciliation bead (gen-2) that depends on ALL the new gap beads. The gen-2 bead re-runs this same workflow to catch anything still missing after the gaps are filled.
   - A gen-2 reconciliation bead may spawn one final gen-3 reconciliation bead using the same pattern. Gen-3 is the hard limit — it must NOT spawn further reconciliation beads.
   - Tag the title with generation: 'Reconcile spec-to-code (gen-N) for ...'
7. Keep this reconciliation bead open (or blocked) until all newly created gap beads (and any follow-up reconciliation bead) are closed.
8. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.
9. If requirements show full coverage, run /opsx:sync to synchronize deltas into the authoritative application specification.
title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage (gen-1) for staffer archetype

description:
Centralize all user-identity-bound integration UX on the Secrets User tab (/secrets),
removing it from the Settings page (/settings). This is part of the credential-authority-model
change that formalizes three-tier credential storage.

## What

Move 6 integration setup cards from SettingsPage IntegrationsCard to the User tab on SecretsPage:
- GoogleOAuthSection (350 lines) — multi-account Google OAuth connect/disconnect
- SpotifySetupCard (567 lines) — Spotify PKCE OAuth flow
- WhatsAppSetupCard (426 lines) + WhatsAppPairModal (304 lines) — QR pairing
- HomeAssistantSetupCard (519 lines) — HA URL + token config
- OwnTracksSetupCard (699 lines) — webhook token generation + setup instructions
- SteamSetupCard (487 lines) — multi-account Steam connect + playtime

Total: ~3,950 lines of self-contained components with own hooks and API calls.

## Why

Currently users must navigate between /settings (for account connections) and /secrets User tab
(for credential management), with no clear guidance about which page to use for what. HA
credentials can even be configured in both places (writing to different stores). Centralizing
user-identity UX on one page eliminates this confusion and prepares for future per-user
credential profiles.

## Architecture

The User tab will have two sections:
1. **Integrations** — the moved cards rendered as-is (above the table)
2. **Credentials** — the existing entity_info table (for manual credential management)

## What stays on Settings
- ModelCatalogCard (ecosystem model config)
- CLIAuthCard (ecosystem LLM API keys + Ollama provider config)
- BlobStorageCard (infrastructure S3 config)
- Appearance, Live Refresh, Command Palette (UI preferences)

Covers: openspec/changes/credential-authority-model/ (unified-secrets-ui spec)
title: Move account-login integration cards from Settings to Secrets User tab

Non-goals: `shared_db_name`/`shared_pool` in credential_store.py and deps.py refer to the credential store topology (shared DB pool concept), not the PostgreSQL schema — those stay.
title: Eliminate shared schema — migrate all tables to public

description:
Owner-level secret configuration (Telegram API hash/ID, Telegram user session,
Home Assistant token, Google OAuth refresh, email password) is currently stored
as secured contact_info entries on the owner contact. This conflates identity
credentials with contact channel identifiers (Telegram chat ID, email address).

Target state:
1. New shared.entity_info table — parallel to contact_info but FK'd to entities.
2. Owner entity (roles=['owner']) bootstrapped on first startup, distinctly
   labelled and editable at /butlers/entities.
3. User-level credentials managed on the Owner entity detail page.
4. Contact-level channel identifiers (telegram chat ID, email address) remain
   on contact_info.

Credential split:
  → entity_info: telegram_api_hash, telegram_api_id, telegram_user_session,
    home_assistant_token, google_oauth_refresh, email_password
  → contact_info (unchanged): telegram (chat ID), email (address), phone,
    telegram_bot_token (butler-level)
title: Move owner secrets from contact_info to entity_info

close_reason: Epic complete — all subtasks implemented, reviewed, merged, and reconciled against spec
description:
Replace the Telegram user-client connector's per-message submission model with
per-chat buffered ingestion that includes conversation history context.

Currently each NewMessage event is normalized and submitted individually.
Butlers receiving these isolated messages lack conversational context, making
factoid extraction unreliable (e.g., "yes, Thursday works" is meaningless
without knowing what Thursday refers to).

This epic adds:
- Per-chat message buffers with a 10-minute configurable flush interval
- Conversation history window (max 50 messages / 30 minutes) fetched on flush
- Single-level reply-to resolution for replied-to messages outside the window
- Batch ingest.v1 envelope with `payload.conversation_history` field
- Force-flush on buffer cap (200 messages) and graceful shutdown

Full spec: openspec/changes/telegram-user-client-conversation-history/
Design: openspec/changes/telegram-user-client-conversation-history/design.md

Env vars introduced:
- TELEGRAM_USER_FLUSH_INTERVAL_S (default: 600)
- TELEGRAM_USER_HISTORY_MAX_MESSAGES (default: 50)
- TELEGRAM_USER_HISTORY_TIME_WINDOW_M (default: 30)
- TELEGRAM_USER_BUFFER_MAX_MESSAGES (default: 200)

Primary file: src/butlers/connectors/telegram_user_client.py
Test file: tests/connectors/test_telegram_user_client.py
title: Add conversation history buffering to Telegram user-client connector

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-x9gm
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:09:28Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The module-contacts spec (Requirement: TelegramContactsProvider, Scenario: Telegram full sync) states:
  external_id = "telegram:<user_id>" (e.g., "telegram:123456789")

But the implementation in telegram_provider.py:83 uses:
  external_id=user_id  (just the bare numeric string, e.g., "123456789")

This affects contacts_source_links uniqueness. While it works correctly in single-provider mode (provider column disambiguates), the bare format is inconsistent with the spec's namespaced convention that prevents external_id collisions across future providers that might also use numeric IDs.

**Fix**: Change telegram_provider.py _user_to_canonical() to use f"telegram:{user_id}" for external_id. Note: existing contacts_source_links rows will need a data migration or the sync engine will create duplicate entries on first run after the fix.

**Files**: src/butlers/modules/contacts/telegram_provider.py:83
title: Telegram external_id format mismatch with spec

description: Replace static per-butler model assignment (hardcoded in butler.toml) with dynamic model selection driven by task complexity. The system picks the right model at spawn time: cheaper/faster models for trivial work, heavier models for complex tasks. Three pillars: (1) Model catalog -- shared.model_catalog table with named aliases, global defaults + per-butler overrides, 12 seed entries across Claude/Codex/Gemini/OpenCode Go. (2) Complexity classification -- 4-tier enum (trivial/medium/high/extra_high), Switchboard classifies during routing, schedulers carry hints. (3) Spawner resolution -- resolve_model(butler, complexity) queries catalog with overrides, lazy adapter pool for cross-runtime invocation. Full spec: openspec/changes/dynamic-model-routing/

description:
Create the database foundation and core resolution logic for dynamic model routing.

Schema:
- shared.model_catalog table (id, alias UNIQUE, runtime_type, model_id, extra_args JSONB, complexity_tier CHECK, enabled, priority, created_at, updated_at)
- shared.butler_model_overrides table (id, butler_name, catalog_entry_id FK CASCADE, enabled, priority, complexity_tier; UNIQUE on butler_name+catalog_entry_id)
- Add complexity column (text, nullable, default 'medium') to scheduled_tasks table
- Seed 12 default entries: claude-haiku/sonnet/opus, gpt-5.1/5.3-spark/5.4/5.4-high, gemini-2.5-flash/pro, minimax-m2.5, glm-5, kimi-k2.5

Resolution:
- Define Complexity enum (trivial/medium/high/extra_high) in src/butlers/core/model_routing.py
- Implement resolve_model(pool, butler_name, complexity_tier) -- single SQL with LEFT JOIN on overrides, COALESCE for nullable fields, ORDER BY effective priority then created_at
- Returns (runtime_type, model_id, extra_args) or None

Tests for resolve_model: global-only, override disable, tier remap, priority override, no-candidates-returns-None, tie-breaking.

description:

- Add complexity: Complexity = Complexity.MEDIUM parameter to trigger() signature
- Add lazy adapter pool (dict[str, RuntimeAdapter]) initialized with TOML adapter; _get_or_create_adapter(runtime_type) method instantiates on demand via get_adapter().create_worker() and caches

description:
Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (openspec/changes/dynamic-model-routing/) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
3. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement to its implementing bead.
4. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
5. If gap beads were created in step 4, create a follow-up reconciliation bead (gen-2) that depends on ALL the new gap beads.
6. Keep this reconciliation bead open until all newly created gap beads are closed.
7. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.
title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage for dynamic model routing (gen-1)

description:
Replace the static per-butler model assignment (hardcoded in butler.toml) with
dynamic model selection driven by task complexity. The system picks the right
model for the job at spawn time: cheaper/faster models for trivial work, heavier
models for complex tasks.

Three pillars:
1. **Model catalog** — shared.model_catalog table with named aliases (e.g.
   gpt-5.4-high = gpt-5.4 + reasoning effort args). Global defaults with
   per-butler overrides via shared.butler_model_overrides. 12 seed entries
   covering Claude, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode Go runtimes.
2. **Complexity classification** — 4-tier enum (trivial/medium/high/extra_high).
   Switchboard classifies complexity during LLM routing (piggybacks on existing
   call). Schedulers carry hints (default medium). Ticks use trivial.
3. **Spawner resolution** — resolve_model(butler, complexity) queries catalog
   with overrides applied, returns (runtime_type, model_id, extra_args).
   Lazy adapter pool allows different runtimes per invocation.

Full spec: openspec/changes/dynamic-model-routing/
title: Dynamic model routing based on task complexity

description:
The contacts module currently only supports Google Contacts as a sync source. Telegram contacts are a valuable identity source — the user's Telegram contact list contains phone numbers, usernames, and chat IDs that enable cross-provider identity resolution and richer disambiguation.

This epic adds Telegram as a second contacts provider, updating the multi-provider architecture and ensuring contacts from both sources are properly disambiguated via phone number and name matching. Telegram-specific fields (username/handle, user ID, chat ID) must be stored in shared.contact_info to support reverse-lookup and Switchboard identity resolution.

The Telethon dependency and user-client credential infrastructure (telegram_api_id, telegram_api_hash, telegram_user_session in shared.contact_info) already exist for the Telegram user client connector — this work extends that foundation to contact syncing.
title: Add Telegram contacts sync provider

description:
Hybrid predicate search MCP tool with three-signal retrieval fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). Inspired by CASS search architecture.

**Schema migration:**
- Enable pg_trgm extension
- Add search_vector (tsvector) with weighted trigger on name (A) + description (B)
- Add description_embedding vector(384) column
- Create GIN indexes: name with gin_trgm_ops, search_vector
- Backfill search_vector and description_embedding for seeded predicates
- Enrich seed descriptions with synonyms (e.g., parent_of → 'father, mother, parent, child relationship')

**Three retrieval signals:**
1. Trigram fuzzy on name (pg_trgm similarity > 0.3) — catches typos
2. Full-text search on name+description (tsvector ts_rank) — handles stemming, multi-word
3. Semantic similarity on description embedding (cosine) — conceptual matching ('dad' → parent_of)

**RRF fusion:** score = SUM(1 / (60 + rank_i)) across all three ranked lists.

Spec reference: openspec/changes/predicate-registry-enforcement/specs/predicate-enforcement/spec.md — Requirements: Predicate registry description and search indexes (4 scenarios), Predicate search MCP tool with hybrid retrieval (7 scenarios).

Added spec: openspec/changes/predicate-registry-enforcement/specs/module-memory/spec.md — ADDED Requirement: memory_predicate_search MCP tool with hybrid retrieval.

Design reference: openspec/changes/predicate-registry-enforcement/design.md — D6: memory_predicate_search uses hybrid retrieval with RRF fusion.

Tasks: 6.1–6.4, 7.1–7.4 from openspec/changes/predicate-registry-enforcement/tasks.md
title: memory_predicate_search MCP tool

Update openspec specifications to define the bitemporal facts model and the meal-to-facts migration.

## Files to update

### openspec/specs/butler-health/spec.md

description:
Reconciliation from bu-axm.7 found that `roster/health/tools/diet.py::_get_owner_entity_id()` still resolves the owner via `shared.contacts.roles @> ["owner"]`. That column was removed in `core_016`, and the repo's owner bootstrap contract is now entity-based (`shared.entities.roles`).

As written, `meal_log()` can fail on migrated databases or silently lose the intended owner entity anchoring for meal facts. This breaks the health-spec requirement that meal facts be attached to the user / owner entity when available.

Scope:
- Replace the stale `shared.contacts.roles` lookup with the canonical owner-entity resolution path used elsewhere in the repo
- Preserve graceful fallback when no owner entity exists yet (meal logging should still work with subject-only facts)
- Add regression coverage for migrated-schema behavior

title: Fix meal_log owner entity lookup after contacts.roles removal

description:
Reconciliation from bu-axm.7 found that the predicate registry schema now includes `is_temporal`, but the exported MCP tool surface does not. `src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/management.py::predicate_list()` still selects only `name`, subject/object types, `is_edge`, and `description`, and orders results by `is_edge, name`.

OpenSpec requires the predicate-list tool to expose `is_temporal` and return rows ordered by `name ASC`. Without this, callers cannot discover which predicates are intended for temporal fact sequences, and the registry contract added by the bitemporal epic is only partially implemented.

Scope:
- Return `is_temporal` from the predicate-list query and MCP surface
- Order results by `name ASC`
- Keep `edges_only` filtering working
- Add/update tests for the tool metadata and returned row shape

description:
Reconciliation from bu-axm.7 found that the butler-health spec requires meal fact metadata to support optional contextual fields beyond calories/macros: `mood_before`, `satisfaction`, `symptom_notes`, and `tags`.

Current `meal_log()` only accepts nutrition, eaten_at, and notes, and only persists `estimated_calories`, `macros`, `logged_at`, `meal_items`, and `notes`. The optional contextual metadata contract added in the spec update was never implemented.

Scope:

The health butler already has the calendar module enabled (modules.calendar in butler.toml). acceptance_criteria:
1. Facts stored without `valid_at` persist `valid_at = NULL` rather than `now()`
2. Property-fact supersession applies only to active facts with the same uniqueness key and `valid_at IS NULL`
3. Temporal facts with different `valid_at` values coexist as active facts even when keys otherwise match
4. Property facts and temporal facts with the same key coexist correctly instead of superseding each other
5. MCP/tool descriptions and tests match the NULL-vs-temporal contract
description:
Reconciliation from bu-axm.7 found the current bitemporal implementation diverges from the OpenSpec contract in module-memory/spec.md. The spec requires `facts.valid_at` to be nullable with default NULL for non-temporal/property facts, and the property-vs-temporal distinction is defined by `valid_at IS NULL` vs `valid_at IS NOT NULL`.

Current code does the opposite:
- mem_007 adds `valid_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`
- `store_fact()` writes `now()` when `valid_at` is omitted
- supersession/coexistence behavior is keyed off `predicate_registry.is_temporal` instead of `valid_at` nullness

This means property facts cannot remain truly non-temporal, MCP/docs claim the wrong default, and the spec's property-vs-temporal coexistence rules are not actually represented in storage semantics.

Scope:
- Make `facts.valid_at` nullable/default NULL for property facts
- Update `store_fact()` / `memory_store_fact` / MCP docs to preserve NULL when omitted
- Base supersession/coexistence on `valid_at` nullness per spec, not registry lookup
- Update or add tests that cover property-vs-temporal coexistence explicitly
notes: Cleanup: Released stale in_progress claim (worktree exists, no commits, no remote branch)
title: Fix facts valid_at NULL semantics for property facts

description: In roster/finance/tools/overview.py subscription_audit(), the last_charge_date lookup uses a per-subscription N+1 query with LIKE match on merchant name. This can match unrelated transactions and is non-SARGable. Fix: pre-fetch all relevant transactions in a single query and perform merchant matching in Python. Consider requiring minimum match length to reduce false positives. Pre-existing from PR #796, flagged during PR #899 review.
title: Fix N+1 LIKE query in subscription_audit for last_charge_date

Need to add conversation_history array to WA batch envelope matching Telegram format. Discovered by bu-dpxb.

description: The GmailConnectorManager health server (GET /health, POST /reload) listens on 0.0.0.0 without authentication. The /health endpoint exposes a list of connected Gmail account emails (PII) and per-account error messages. The /reload endpoint allows unauthenticated triggering of DB re-scan. Options: (1) bind to 127.0.0.1 only in uvicorn config, (2) add bearer token auth to /reload, (3) redact email addresses in health response (hash or partial mask). Current deployment relies on infrastructure-level port mapping to restrict access, but in-process hardening would provide defense in depth. Discovered during PR review of #572.
title: Harden Gmail connector manager health endpoint

description:
core_014 migrated entity data into shared.entities and re-pointed FKs, but left empty per-butler entities tables in place. These shadow shared.entities via search_path, causing the dashboard entities list to miss entities that only exist in shared (e.g. the Owner entity linked to the owner contact).

Additionally, general.entities is a completely different table (freeform collection items with collection_id, data, tags columns) that collides by name.

Goal: make shared.entities the sole source of identity entities. Per-butler overlays (facts, contact_info, entity_info) continue to reference shared.entities via FK.

Scope:
1. Rename general.entities -> general.collection_items to resolve naming collision
2. Drop empty per-butler entities shadow tables (education, finance, health, home, messenger, relationship, switchboard, travel)
3. Update memory API router to query shared.entities directly instead of fan-out
4. Update memory MCP tools to explicitly qualify shared.entities

Non-goals:
- Changing facts or entity_info table locations (already correctly FK'd to shared.entities)
- Changing the search_path mechanism itself
title: Consolidate entities to shared.entities as single source of truth

description:
general.entities is a freeform collection-items table (collection_id FK, data JSONB, tags JSONB) that collides with the identity entities table name. This shadowing must be resolved before dropping other per-butler entities tables.

Migration (new Alembic version under roster/general/migrations/):
- ALTER TABLE general.entities RENAME TO general.collection_items
- Rename indexes: idx_entities_data_gin -> idx_collection_items_data_gin, idx_entities_collection_id -> idx_collection_items_collection_id
- Update any FK references

Code updates:
- roster/general/tools/entities.py — update all SQL references from 'entities' to 'collection_items'
- roster/general/tools/collections.py — update any references
- roster/general/api/router.py — update any SQL
- roster/general/api/models.py — update if needed
- roster/general/modules/tools.py and __init__.py — update if needed
- tests — update test SQL
title: Rename general.entities to general.collection_items

description:
After general.entities is renamed, drop the remaining per-butler entities tables that shadow shared.entities. These tables are empty (or contain stale duplicates already in shared.entities) and prevent the dashboard entities list from finding entities.

Migration (new core Alembic version):
- For each schema in (education, finance, health, home, messenger, relationship, switchboard, travel): DROP TABLE IF EXISTS {schema}.entities
- Guard with existence checks; downgrade should re-create empty tables matching mem_002 schema

API changes in src/butlers/api/routers/memory.py:
- Replace _fan_out_memory_queries for entity list/detail/search with a single direct query to shared.entities (using any available pool, since shared.entities is accessible from all)
- Entity list endpoint (list_entities): query shared.entities directly instead of merging results from per-butler pools
- Entity detail endpoint: query shared.entities directly
- Entity search: query shared.entities directly
- Keep fan-out pattern for facts/episodes/rules (those remain per-butler)

Acceptance criteria:
1. Per-butler entities tables no longer exist (except general.collection_items)
2. GET /api/memory/entities returns all entities from shared.entities including Owner
3. GET /api/memory/entities/{id} works for any entity in shared.entities
4. Facts queries still work (FK to shared.entities is unaffected)
5. No regression in entity creation/update via MCP tools
title: Drop per-butler entities shadow tables and query shared.entities directly

description:
Memory module MCP tools in src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/entities.py use unqualified 'entities' table references. After dropping per-butler shadow tables, search_path fallback will make these work, but explicitly qualifying as shared.entities is more robust and self-documenting.

Files:
- src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/entities.py — update all SQL: FROM entities -> FROM shared.entities, INSERT INTO entities -> INSERT INTO shared.entities, UPDATE entities -> UPDATE shared.entities
- src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/facts.py — check for any entity joins
- Any other memory module files that reference unqualified entities table

This is a hardening/clarity change, not a correctness fix (search_path fallback works after shadow tables are dropped).
title: Qualify entity MCP tool queries with shared.entities

description:
The discord connector (src/butlers/connectors/discord_user.py) does not have source filter integration. The connector is marked as DRAFT/v2-only but has basic channel filtering via DISCORD_CHANNEL_ALLOWLIST env var.

## What needs to be implemented
- Instantiate SourceFilterEvaluator with connector_type='discord' and pass db_pool (if available)
- Call ensure_loaded() before the Gateway ingestion loop
- Apply source filter gate in message processing using key_type='channel_id'
- Extract str(channel_id) from Discord message event as the filter key
- Return/skip blocked messages without calling Switchboard
- Non-channel_id key types should be skipped per the shared module's WARNING logic
- Requires db_pool wiring in the connector (currently discord_user.py has no DB pool)

## Context
The gap note in bu-qbq.7 identified this explicitly: 'discord connector (key_type: channel_id) — verify if implemented or needs new child bead'. Code audit confirmed it is NOT implemented.

The connector_source_filters table already includes connector_type='discord' in the valid key types map (_CONNECTOR_VALID_KEY_TYPES in router.py at line 2744) with key type 'channel_id', so the API side is ready.

Note: discord_user.py is a DRAFT connector ('v2-only WIP, not production-ready'). This task should be implemented when the discord connector is promoted to production readiness.

## Acceptance criteria
1. SourceFilterEvaluator instantiated in DiscordUserConnector (with optional db_pool for fail-open)
2. ensure_loaded() called at startup
3. channel_id extracted from Discord Gateway message events; blocked messages skip Switchboard
4. Tests for the filter gate in test_discord_user_connector.py
title: discord connector: source filter gate on channel_id

description:
Rewrite src/butlers/core/runtimes/claude_code.py to invoke the `claude` CLI binary
via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec instead of the claude_agent_sdk Python library.

Covers: openspec/changes/claude-binary-adapter/specs/core-spawner/spec.md — all
MODIFIED scenarios for "Claude Code adapter invocation via subprocess", "binary not
found", "process timeout", "non-zero exit", "MCP config isolation", "token usage
extraction", "tool call extraction", "environment isolation", "max_turns parameter",
"worker creation", "config file generation", "system prompt file reading".

Implementation (from design.md decisions D1-D9):
1. Add _find_claude_binary() — shutil.which("claude") with FileNotFoundError + install hint
2. Add _parse_claude_output(stdout, stderr, returncode) — parse stream-json JSON-lines:
   - result events → result text + usage (input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read/creation)
   - assistant messages with tool_use content blocks → tool calls (id, name, input)
   - Non-zero exit → RuntimeError with stderr
3. Remove all claude_agent_sdk imports (ClaudeAgentOptions, query, ResultMessage, ToolUseBlock, McpSSEServerConfig, McpHttpServerConfig)
4. Rewrite __init__() — accept claude_binary: str | None, keep butler_name/log_root for stderr logging, add _last_process_info
5. Implement last_process_info property returning {pid, exit_code, command, stderr, runtime_type}
6. Rewrite invoke() — build command: claude -p --output-format stream-json --bare --no-session-persistence --permission-mode bypassPermissions --strict-mcp-config --system-prompt <prompt> --mcp-config <path> [--model <model>] [runtime_args...] -- <prompt>
   - Write temp MCP config via build_config_file() in tempfile.TemporaryDirectory
   - Spawn subprocess, capture stdout/stderr, enforce timeout via asyncio.wait_for
   - Parse output via _parse_claude_output()
   - Capture stderr to {log_root}/butlers/{butler_name}_cc_stderr.log
7. Update create_worker() to return new instance with same claude_binary, butler_name, log_root
8. Keep build_config_file() unchanged (already writes mcp.json compatible with --mcp-config)
9. Keep parse_system_prompt_file() unchanged (reads CLAUDE.md)

Reference: CodexAdapter (src/butlers/core/runtimes/codex.py) as the structural template.
title: Rewrite ClaudeCodeAdapter invoke() from SDK to subprocess

description:
Implement Telethon-based history fetching and single-level reply-to resolution,
called during the flush pipeline to provide conversation context.

Covers spec sections:
- Requirement: Conversation History Window (all scenarios)
- Requirement: Reply-To Resolution (all scenarios)

Work:
1. Implement _fetch_conversation_history(chat_id, buffered_messages) -> list[Message]:
   - Call client.get_messages() with limit=HISTORY_MAX_MESSAGES and
     offset_date bounded by HISTORY_TIME_WINDOW_M before oldest buffered message
   - Merge history + buffered messages, deduplicate by message ID
   - Sort by message ID ascending
2. Add FloodWaitError handling: catch, log warning, proceed without history (fail-open)
3. Add sequential delay between chat flushes to avoid Telegram rate limits
4. Implement _resolve_reply_tos(chat_id, buffered_messages, context_messages) -> list[Message]:
   - Collect reply_to_msg_id from buffered messages
   - Filter out IDs already present in context_messages
   - Fetch missing via client.get_messages(chat, ids=mid)
   - Append to context, fail-open on fetch errors
5. Single-level resolution only — no recursive chain following

These methods are pure fetchers consumed by the flush pipeline (child 3).

File: src/butlers/connectors/telegram_user_client.py
Spec: openspec/changes/telegram-user-client-conversation-history/specs/telegram-user-client-conversation-history/spec.md
title: Add conversation history fetching and reply-to resolution

description:
## Problem

When a message arrives from Telegram and is routed to a butler (e.g. travel), the butler's
LLM runtime calls memory_entity_resolve(name="Owner", tenant_id="owner") to anchor facts —
a fuzzy string match that:
1. Uses the wrong tenant_id ("owner" instead of "shared"), creating entities invisible to
   the dashboard (which filters tenant_id IN ('default', 'shared'))
2. Relies on name guessing instead of using the pre-resolved entity_id already present in
   request_context.source_sender_entity_id

The identity resolution pipeline already does the hard work:
- Switchboard resolves telegram chat_id → contact_info → contact → entity
- route_to_butler injects source_sender_entity_id into request_context
- The identity preamble includes entity_id in the prompt text

But the LLM ignores all of this and invents its own resolution path. This is fragile and
produces orphaned facts detached from the knowledge graph.

## Root causes

1. **Spec gap**: The module-memory spec's entity tool registration (line 676) says
   entity_resolve defaults tenant_id to 'default', contradicting entity-identity spec
   which mandates tenant_id='shared' for the owner entity. (Code fix already applied —
   all entity MCP tools now default tenant_id='shared'.)

2. **No structured entity_id handoff**: The switchboard-identity spec says butlers "MUST
   use entity_id as the anchor when storing facts" but this relies on the LLM parsing
   a text preamble like `[Source: Owner (contact_id: X, entity_id: Y), via telegram]`.
   LLMs frequently ignore or misparse this.

3. **No documentation gap coverage**: The memory spec doesn't describe how entity_id
   should flow from request_context into memory_store_fact calls. The switchboard-identity
   spec describes the preamble but not the structured data path.

## Desired outcome

- Sender's entity_id reliably reaches memory_store_fact without LLM string-parsing
- All entity MCP tools default to tenant_id='shared' (spec + code aligned)
- Dashboard entity pages show facts regardless of which butler stored them
- OpenSpecs updated to document the full entity_id flow from ingestion to memory storage
title: Ensure sender entity_id flows reliably from ingestion to memory storage

description:
Implement the QA Staffer as defined in openspec/changes/qa-staffer/.

A permanently-running infrastructure staffer (type="staffer") that subsumes per-butler
self-healing into a unified, system-wide quality assurance function. Key capabilities:

- Pluggable DiscoverySource protocol with 3 v1 sources (log scanner, session records
  via RFC 0010 SQL view, butler reports via Switchboard route() relay)
- Source-agnostic triage with fingerprint dedup against active investigations, dismissals, cooldowns
- Investigation dispatch reusing existing healing infrastructure (worktree, anonymizer, PR pipeline)
- Dashboard at /qa with known issues tracker, patrol drill-down, investigation timeline, PR links
- Self-healing module migrated to relay via Switchboard route() with fallback
- Sandboxed investigation agents with Tier 1 CredentialStore credentials
- OTel tracing + Prometheus metrics per RFC 0005
- All open questions resolved (no qa.log self-scan, current file only, unified labels, 30-day fallback sunset)

Spec: openspec/changes/qa-staffer/ (proposal.md, design.md, 8 delta specs, tasks.md)
notes:
**Development protocol (all workers must follow):**
- TDD: Write failing tests FIRST, then implement. Per development.md: "New features need tests before merge."
- Quality gate before PR: `uv run ruff check src/ tests/ --output-format concise && uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/ -q && uv run pytest tests/ -q --maxfail=1 --tb=short`
- Manifesto-driven: Before adding any MCP tool, verify it aligns with the governing document (MANIFESTO.md for staffers).
- OpenSpec-anchored: Each bead cites specific spec sections. Workers must read cited sections before implementing and verify against them after.
title: Implement QA Staffer — system-wide quality assurance agent

description:
When memory_entity_resolve returns no matches during fact storage, agents currently fall back to legacy string-subject anchoring — the fact's subject is a bare string with no entity_id. These facts are invisible in /entities and cannot be merged, linked, or promoted.

This epic implements the resolve-or-create-transitory pattern: when entity resolution fails, the agent must create a transitory entity (metadata.unidentified=true) via entity_create and anchor the fact to it. This reuses the existing unidentified entity pattern from contacts, making the entity visible in the dashboard's Unidentified Entities section for user review (merge, confirm, or delete).

No tool code changes needed — entity_create, entity_resolve, and store_fact already support the mechanics. The change is to the documented contract (specs, skills, predicate taxonomy) and a backfill of existing string-anchored facts.

OpenSpec change: openspec/changes/transitory-entity-on-fact-storage/
title: Implement transitory entity creation on memory fact storage

description:
Merge the delta specs from openspec/changes/transitory-entity-on-fact-storage/ into the main specs, and update the CRUD-to-SPO predicate taxonomy to require metadata.unidentified=true on auto-created entities.

Work items:
1. Run openspec sync to merge delta specs into openspec/specs/module-memory/spec.md (MODIFIED: Mandatory entity anchoring requirement extended to all entity types) and openspec/specs/entity-identity/spec.md (ADDED: Transitory entity convention requirement).
2. Update openspec/changes/crud-to-spo-migration/specs/predicate-taxonomy.md §1.2 Resolution Cascade — add metadata.unidentified=true requirement to the Unresolved actors row, add new row for Unknown organization/place from ingestion.
3. Update §1.4 Contact Entity Resolution for Relationship Data to include unidentified metadata flag when creating entities for contacts with no entity_id.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Main specs reflect the merged delta content (entity-identity has transitory entity convention, module-memory has broadened entity anchoring requirement)
2. Predicate taxonomy Resolution Cascade table includes unidentified metadata requirement for all auto-created entities
3. openspec status shows the change as synced
title: Sync transitory entity specs and align predicate taxonomy

description:
Update the shared butler-memory skill (roster/shared/skills/butler-memory/SKILL.md) to extend the resolve-or-create pattern from person-only to ALL external entities (organizations, places, other).

Current state: The skill already documents entity resolution for 'mentioned people' with the resolve → create → use flow. But it only covers entity_type=person and does not specify metadata.unidentified=true on creation.

Changes needed:
1. Broaden the 'Entity Resolution for Mentioned People' section to cover ALL external entities (merchants, organizations, places, etc.), not just people.
2. Mandate metadata.unidentified=true plus source provenance (source: fact_storage, source_butler, source_scope) on every entity_create call when resolution returns zero candidates.
3. Add entity_type inference guidance: merchant/company → organization, person name → person, location → place, unknown → other.
4. Add idempotency handling: if entity_create raises unique constraint violation, catch and resolve the existing entity instead of failing.
5. Add an example showing the organization/merchant flow (like the Nutrition Kitchen SG case).

Acceptance criteria:
1. Skill covers entity resolution for all entity types, not just person
2. metadata.unidentified=true is mandated on all auto-created entities
3. Source provenance metadata is documented with example
4. Idempotency handling for duplicate names is documented
5. Entity type inference guidance is included
title: Update butler-memory skill with resolve-or-create-transitory protocol

description:
Find and backfill all existing facts stored with bare string subjects (entity_id IS NULL) by creating transitory entities and linking them.

Steps:
1. Write a diagnostic query against each butler schema's facts table to find active facts where entity_id IS NULL and subject is not a known generic label (like 'Owner'). Report the count and distinct (subject, scope) pairs.
2. Write a migration script (Python, using asyncpg) that for each unique (subject, scope) pair:
   a. Infers entity_type from the subject string and scope (e.g., finance scope merchant names → organization)
   b. Creates a transitory entity via INSERT INTO shared.entities with metadata={unidentified: true, source: backfill, source_scope: <scope>}
   c. Updates all matching facts to set entity_id to the new entity's UUID
   d. Handles unique constraint violations (entity may already exist) by resolving the existing entity
3. Run the backfill and verify affected facts now appear linked in /entities.

Known case: 'Nutrition Kitchen SG' in finance scope — stored as string subject by the finance butler's email processing.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Diagnostic query identifies all string-anchored facts across butler schemas
2. Migration script creates transitory entities with correct metadata and entity_type
3. All previously string-anchored facts now have entity_id set
4. Created entities appear in /entities Unidentified Entities section
5. No data loss — subject field preserved as human-readable label

description:
Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (see epic description and openspec/changes/transitory-entity-on-fact-storage/) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description, all OpenSpec artifacts (proposal, design, specs, tasks), and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
3. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement to its implementing bead.
4. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
   c. Do NOT create another reconciliation bead.
5. Keep this reconciliation bead open (or blocked) until all newly created gap beads are closed.
6. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Every requirement in the OpenSpec delta specs has a corresponding implementation bead
2. Any gaps found result in new child beads created under the same epic
3. Reconciliation summary is recorded in the close reason
title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage for transitory entity on fact storage

description:
Create the QA staffer roster entry, implement the QA module, and migrate the self-healing
module to relay findings via Switchboard.

Covers openspec/changes/qa-staffer/ tasks 5.* + 6.* + 13.0:

**Roster entry (roster/qa/):**
- butler.toml: type="staffer", cross_butler_access=["*"], schedules, [modules.qa] config
- MANIFESTO.md: infrastructure contract (responsibilities, SLAs, failure modes, escalation)
- CLAUDE.md: system prompt for QA staffer LLM investigation sessions
- AGENTS.md: initialized "# Notes to self"

**QA module (src/butlers/modules/qa/__init__.py):**
- Module ABC: register_tools (report_finding, force_patrol, get_qa_status),
  on_startup (register sources, reap stale worktrees, recover stale patrols + dispatch_pending),
  on_shutdown (cancel watchdogs, NOT active sessions — those drain via daemon phase 3),
  migration_revisions()=None, dependencies=[], tool_metadata (context sensitive on report_finding)
- Patrol loop: scheduler-driven tick, asyncio.Lock overlap prevention, poll sources, triage, dispatch
- Mini-patrol for severity-0 reactive findings
- Patrol crash recovery: stale "running" rows cleaned on restart
- OTel spans: qa.patrol parent, qa.discover.<source> children, qa.triage, qa.dispatch, qa.investigation root
- Prometheus: qa_patrol_total, qa_findings_total, qa_investigations_active, qa_patrol_duration_seconds,
  qa_investigation_duration_seconds (low-cardinality per RFC 0005)

**Self-healing relay migration (tasks 5.*):**
- Refactor modules/self_healing/ to relay via switchboard_client.call_tool("route",
  {"target_butler": "qa", "tool_name": "report_finding"}) with allow_stale=True
- Detect QA availability via cached list_butlers (TTL-based)
- Route failure → fallback to direct dispatch, logged at WARNING
- Preserve fallback path for standalone deployments
- Deprecate gh_token_env_var → CredentialStore.resolve("BUTLERS_QA_GH_TOKEN") (task 13.0)
- Add qa_fallback_activations_total Prometheus counter (task 13.9)

**Tests for patrol loop, relay behavior, fallback paths.**

Covers: specs/staffer-qa (all requirements), specs/self-healing-module (all requirements)
notes:
**Manifesto-first workflow:** Write roster/qa/MANIFESTO.md BEFORE implementing any MCP tools. Per development.md: "Before adding tool, check manifesto support. Update manifesto first, then code." Validate report_finding, force_patrol, get_qa_status against the manifesto before registering them.

**Self-healing relay:** The Switchboard route() tool calls report_finding directly on the QA staffer's MCP server (tool-to-tool, not route.execute which spawns a session). This is lightweight and synchronous — no LLM session involved in the relay path. Use allow_stale=True on the route call as a safety net.
title: Implement QA module, roster entry, and self-healing relay migration

description:
The fix/switchboard-routing-log-schema-drift branch (PR #508) fixes the original thread_id schema drift in routing_log test fixtures, but unmasking that failure reveals additional pre-existing failures on main that the --maxfail=5 CI flag was hiding.

**Background**

CI runs with --maxfail=5. The original failure (5x 'column thread_id does not exist' in test_route_* tests) saturated the limit, so all failures beneath were never reached. Each wave of fixes exposes the next layer.

**Failure waves observed so far**

Wave 1 (fixed in PR #508, commit by beads-coordinator):
- test_route_to_unknown_butler et al — UndefinedColumnError: column 'thread_id' of relation 'routing_log' does not exist
- Root cause: routing_log DDL in test fixture missing columns from migrations 021 and 023
- Fix: added thread_id, source_channel, contact_id, entity_id, sender_roles to CREATE TABLE

Wave 2 (fixed in PR #508, subsequent commit):
- test_deliver_telegram_success, test_deliver_email_success et al — assert 'failed' == 'sent'
- Root cause: deliver() refactored to require notify.v1 envelope for non-switchboard callers; tests still used old direct-channel interface
- Fix: updated tests to pass notify_request dict with schema_version='notify.v1'

- test_education::TestListQuizResponses::test_returns_quiz_response_data — KeyError: 'meta'
- Root cause: _quiz_response_record() fixture missing evaluator_notes and node_label fields
- Fix: added missing fields as None

Wave 3 (now blocking PR #508):
- roster/general/tests/test_tools.py::test_collection_* — InterfaceError: cannot perform operation: another operation is in progress
- Root cause: pre-existing asyncpg event-loop isolation issue in general butler test fixtures; not introduced by PR #508 (file not in diff)
- Status: unresolved

**Task**

Investigate and fix each remaining CI failure on the fix/switchboard-routing-log-schema-drift branch (PR #508). For each failure:
1. Determine if it is introduced by the branch or pre-existing on main (git diff main..HEAD --name-only)
2. If pre-existing: fix it anyway so the PR is green (pre-existing failures on main must be cleaned up to merge)
3. If introduced: fix the root cause

Run the full CI suite locally to catch all remaining waves before each push:
- make lint
- uv run pytest tests/ -q --tb=short --ignore=tests/test_db.py --ignore=tests/test_migrations.py --ignore=tests/e2e -m 'not integration and not e2e'
- TESTCONTAINERS_RYUK_DISABLED=true uv run pytest roster/ tests/integration/ -q --tb=short -m 'integration' -n auto --dist loadfile

Do NOT use --maxfail when doing the full sweep — you need to see all failures at once.
notes:
Branch: fix/switchboard-routing-log-schema-drift | PR: https://github.com/Tzeusy/butlers/pull/508 | Known current blocker: roster/general/tests/test_tools.py test_collection_* — asyncpg InterfaceError. File not in PR diff so pre-existing on main. Worker should run full CI without --maxfail to see all remaining waves before pushing.
Worker killed without progress. Re-dispatching.
All branch-introduced failures fixed. Integration test failures are pre-existing on main.
title: Fix cascading CI failures on fix/switchboard-routing-log-schema-drift

Tests to write (in roster/lifestyle/tests/ or tests/ as appropriate):
1. Butler startup test: lifestyle butler loads with configured modules (memory, calendar, contacts), all standard tools registered
2. Memory taxonomy test: store and retrieve taste preference facts using the domain taxonomy (likes_genre, likes_artist, likes_cuisine, favorite_restaurant, hobby, food_preference, food_dislike, routine, watches, reads)

description:
Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (see epic description and openspec/changes/lifestyle-butler/) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
3. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement to its implementing bead.
4. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
5. If gap beads were created in step 4, create a follow-up reconciliation bead (gen-2) that depends on ALL the new gap beads.
6. Keep this reconciliation bead open until all newly created gap beads are closed.
7. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.
8. Once fully signed off: run /opsx:sync to synchronize deltas into the authoritative application specification, then run /opsx:archive to archive the lifestyle-butler change.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Every requirement in specs/butler-lifestyle/spec.md has a corresponding implementation bead
2. Every requirement in specs/butler-switchboard/spec.md (delta) has a corresponding implementation bead
3. Any gaps found result in new child beads created under the same epic
4. If gaps were found, a follow-up reconciliation bead (gen-2) was created
5. /opsx:sync executed after full coverage confirmed
6. /opsx:archive executed to finalize the change
7. Reconciliation summary is recorded in the close reason
title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage (gen-1) for lifestyle butler

description:
Systematic migration of dedicated CRUD storage tables across butlers to use the bitemporal SPO facts system (from bu-axm). Once bu-axm lands valid_at + is_temporal support on the facts table, other domain tables that store event logs or simple property data can be rewritten as fact wrappers — gaining cross-butler visibility, semantic search, entity anchoring, and lifecycle management for free.

## Entity Anchoring Contract

All facts MUST be anchored to a resolved entity_id via memory_entity_resolve(). Subjects are NEVER generic strings like "user" — they are either:
- A resolved entity UUID (owner, a known contact, a named person)
- An anonymous placeholder entity created via memory_entity_create() for unresolved actors

The owner entity is bootstrapped at daemon startup and available via shared.contacts WHERE roles @> '["owner"]'. All self-referencing health/finance data uses the owner's entity_id.

## Scope — Tables to Migrate

### Phase 1: Health Butler (depends on bu-axm)
Already storing temporal event data with simple schemas:
- measurements → entity_id + measurement_{type} predicate + value in metadata + valid_at
- symptoms → entity_id + symptom predicate + severity/notes in metadata + valid_at  
- medication_doses → entity_id + took_dose predicate + medication ref in metadata + valid_at
- medications → entity_id + medication predicate + dosage/frequency/schedule in metadata (property fact, supersession)
- conditions → entity_id + condition predicate + status in metadata (property fact, supersession)
- research → entity_id + research predicate + content/tags/source_url in metadata (property fact)

### Phase 2: Relationship Butler
Contact-anchored data that maps naturally to entity-anchored facts:
- quick_facts → already SPO-shaped (contact → key → value); migrate to entity_id + predicate + content
- interactions → entity_id + interaction_{type} predicate + summary + valid_at
- life_events → entity_id + life_event predicate + summary + valid_at
- notes → entity_id + note predicate + content + valid_at (append-only temporal)
- gifts → entity_id + gift predicate + description/status in metadata (property fact)
- loans → entity_id + loan predicate + amount/direction in metadata (property fact)
- tasks → entity_id + task predicate + title/completed in metadata (property fact)
- reminders → entity_id + reminder predicate + message/cron in metadata (property fact)
- activity_feed → entity_id + activity predicate + description + valid_at (temporal)

### Phase 3: Finance Butler
Transaction and account data with entity anchoring:
- transactions → owner entity_id + transaction_{direction} predicate + merchant/amount/category in metadata + valid_at=posted_at (temporal)
- accounts → owner entity_id + account predicate + institution/type/last_four in metadata (property fact)
- subscriptions → owner entity_id + subscription predicate + service/amount/status in metadata (property fact)
- bills → owner entity_id + bill predicate + payee/amount/due_date in metadata (property fact)

### Phase 4: Home Butler
- ha_entity_snapshot → entity_id (HA device entity) + ha_state predicate + state/attributes in metadata (property fact with supersession on each snapshot)

### NOT migrating (structural/relational/operational)
- Education mind maps (graph structure with spaced repetition)
- Travel trips/legs/accommodations (parent-child hierarchy)
- Delivery pipeline (state machines with retry logic)
- Approval rules/pending actions (state machines)
- Sync infrastructure (cursor/etag tracking)
- Configuration registries (triage rules, routing instructions)
- Taxonomy seed tables (life_event_categories, relationship_types)

## Predicate Registry Seeding

Each phase seeds new predicates into predicate_registry with is_temporal flag:
- Temporal: measurement_weight, measurement_blood_pressure, symptom, took_dose, interaction_call, life_event, transaction_debit, transaction_credit, etc.
- Property: medication, condition, research, account, subscription, bill, gift, loan, task, reminder, ha_state, etc.

## Migration Strategy

Each table migration follows this pattern:
1. Spec update (openspec delta)
2. Seed predicates into registry (migration)
3. Rewrite tool functions as fact wrappers (query facts table instead of dedicated table)
4. Update MCP tool registrations
5. Data migration script (backfill existing rows into facts)
6. Deprecate old table (do not drop yet)
7. Update tests

## Aggregation Queries

Tools like nutrition_summary, spending_summary, trend_report currently use SQL aggregation on dedicated tables. After migration, these become metadata JSONB queries on facts:
  SELECT metadata->>'calories' FROM facts WHERE predicate LIKE 'meal_%' AND valid_at BETWEEN ...
Performance consideration: add a GIN index on facts.metadata for JSONB queries, and consider a partial index on predicate for high-volume temporal predicates.

## Out of Scope
- Dropping old tables (deprecate only — drop in a future cleanup epic)
- Changing the memory consolidation engine for temporal facts
- Migrating education, travel, delivery, or approval tables
title: Migrate naive CRUD tables to bitemporal SPO facts

description:
Write the specification defining how each CRUD table maps to SPO facts. Covers:

1. Predicate taxonomy — full list of predicates per domain (health, relationship, finance, home), with is_temporal flag, expected_subject_type, expected_object_type, and description.

2. Metadata schema conventions — standardize how structured data (JSONB nutrition, NUMERIC amounts, INTEGER severity) is stored in fact metadata. Define a metadata schema per predicate family (e.g., measurement predicates always have {value: ..., unit: ...}).

3. Entity resolution contract — document that subjects MUST be entity_id-anchored (owner entity for self-data, resolved contact entity for contact-data, anonymous placeholder for unresolved). No generic 'user' subjects.

4. Aggregation query patterns — document how tools like nutrition_summary, spending_summary, trend_report query facts with metadata JSONB extraction. Include index recommendations.

5. Wrapper API contract — each migrated tool must return the same response shape as before (backward compatible). Document the mapping from fact fields to legacy response fields.

Output: delta specs for openspec/specs/module-memory/spec.md and each affected butler spec.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Every table in Phase 1-4 has a defined predicate mapping
2. Metadata schemas are documented per predicate family
3. Entity resolution contract is explicit (no 'user' subjects)
4. Aggregation query patterns are documented with index recommendations
5. Backward-compatible response shapes are defined
title: Spec: define predicate taxonomy and migration patterns for CRUD-to-SPO

description:
Migrate 6 remaining health tables to bitemporal SPO facts. All facts anchored to owner entity_id (resolved from shared.contacts WHERE roles @> '["owner"]').

Temporal tables (event-per-row, is_temporal=true predicates):
- measurements → predicate measurement_{type} (measurement_weight, measurement_blood_pressure, measurement_heart_rate, measurement_blood_sugar, measurement_temperature). Content = human-readable summary. Metadata = {value: <JSONB>, unit: <str>, notes: <str>}. valid_at = measured_at.
- symptoms → predicate symptom. Content = symptom name. Metadata = {severity: <int 1-10>, condition_id: <uuid|null>, notes: <str>}. valid_at = occurred_at.
- medication_doses → predicate took_dose. Content = medication name. Metadata = {medication_id: <uuid>, skipped: <bool>, notes: <str>}. valid_at = taken_at.

Property tables (one-active-per-entity, supersession, is_temporal=false):
- medications → predicate medication. Content = "{name} {dosage} {frequency}". Metadata = {name, dosage, frequency, schedule: <list>, active: <bool>, notes}. Subject = medication name for searchability.
- conditions → predicate condition. Content = "{name}: {status}". Metadata = {name, status, diagnosed_at, notes}. Subject = condition name.
- research → predicate research. Content = research content text. Metadata = {title, tags: <list>, source_url, condition_id}. Subject = research title.

For each table:
1. Seed predicates into predicate_registry (new migration)
2. Rewrite tool functions in roster/health/tools/ as fact wrappers
3. Update MCP tool registrations in roster/health/modules/tools.py
4. Update report tools (health_summary, trend_report) to query facts
5. Update tests in roster/health/tests/test_tools.py
6. Write data migration script for existing rows

Acceptance criteria:
1. All 6 tables' tools read/write facts instead of dedicated tables
2. Predicate registry seeded with all health predicates
3. measurement_history, symptom_history, medication_history return same response shapes
4. health_summary and trend_report work against facts
5. All existing tests pass with updated implementations
title: Migrate health butler CRUD tables to SPO facts (non-meal)

close_reason: Implementation merged to main via PR #503. All relationship butler CRUD tables now write/read SPO facts from the bitemporal facts table.
description:
Migrate 9 relationship butler tables to bitemporal SPO facts. All facts anchored to the relevant contact's entity_id (resolved via shared.contacts → shared.entities FK, NOT generic 'user').

Temporal tables (is_temporal=true):
- interactions → predicate interaction_{type}. Content = summary. Metadata = {type, notes}. valid_at = occurred_at. entity_id = contact's entity.
- life_events → predicate life_event. Content = summary. Metadata = {life_event_type, description}. valid_at = happened_at. entity_id = contact's entity.
- notes → predicate contact_note. Content = note content. Metadata = {emotion}. valid_at = created_at. entity_id = contact's entity. (Append-only, no supersession.)
- activity_feed → predicate activity. Content = description. Metadata = {type}. valid_at = created_at. entity_id = contact's entity.

Property tables (is_temporal=false, supersession):
- quick_facts → predicate = the key itself (dynamic predicate). Content = value. entity_id = contact's entity. (Already SPO-shaped — most natural migration.)
- gifts → predicate gift. Content = description. Metadata = {occasion, status}. entity_id = contact's entity.
- loans → predicate loan. Content = description. Metadata = {amount, direction, settled, settled_at}. entity_id = contact's entity.
- tasks → predicate contact_task. Content = title. Metadata = {description, completed, completed_at}. entity_id = contact's entity.
- reminders → predicate reminder. Content = message. Metadata = {reminder_type, cron, due_at, dismissed}. entity_id = contact's entity.

Entity resolution: Each contact_id FK maps to a shared.contacts row which has an entity_id FK to shared.entities. The fact's entity_id is this resolved entity UUID.

Acceptance criteria:
1. All 9 tables' tools read/write facts instead of dedicated tables
2. Predicate registry seeded with relationship predicates
3. All tool response shapes preserved (backward compatible)
4. quick_facts migration is cleanest (key→predicate, value→content)
5. Tests updated and passing

title: Migrate relationship butler CRUD tables to SPO facts

description:
Migrate 4 finance butler tables to bitemporal SPO facts. All facts anchored to owner entity_id for self-owned financial data.

Temporal tables (is_temporal=true):
- transactions → predicate transaction_{direction} (transaction_debit, transaction_credit). Content = "{merchant} {amount} {currency}". Metadata = {account_id, source_message_id, merchant, description, amount (string-encoded NUMERIC), currency, category, payment_method, receipt_url, external_ref, extra metadata}. valid_at = posted_at. entity_id = owner entity.

Property tables (is_temporal=false, supersession):
- accounts → predicate account. Content = "{institution} {type} ****{last_four}". Metadata = {institution, type, name, last_four, currency, extra metadata}. entity_id = owner entity. Uniqueness key = entity_id + scope + predicate + content (since multiple accounts exist — may need per-account entity or content-based differentiation).
- subscriptions → predicate subscription. Content = "{service} {amount}/{frequency}". Metadata = {service, amount, currency, frequency, next_renewal, status, auto_renew, payment_method, account_id, source_message_id}. entity_id = owner entity.
- bills → predicate bill. Content = "{payee} {amount} due {due_date}". Metadata = {payee, amount, currency, due_date, frequency, status, payment_method, account_id, statement_period_start/end, paid_at, source_message_id}. entity_id = owner entity.

Key challenges:
- Transaction deduplication: current uq_transactions_dedupe partial index on (source_message_id, merchant, amount, posted_at). Need equivalent dedup logic in store_fact or a metadata-based unique constraint.
- NUMERIC precision: transaction amounts are NUMERIC(14,2). Storing as string in metadata JSONB loses type safety. Consider storing amount as string and parsing in aggregation queries.
- spending_summary aggregation: currently SQL SUM/GROUP BY on transactions table. After migration, becomes JSONB extraction + aggregation on facts.

Acceptance criteria:
1. All 4 tables' tools read/write facts instead of dedicated tables
2. Transaction deduplication preserved (no duplicate ingestion from same source message)
3. spending_summary returns correct aggregations from fact metadata
4. NUMERIC precision maintained through string encoding in metadata
5. Tests updated and passing
title: Migrate finance butler CRUD tables to SPO facts

description:
Migrate ha_entity_snapshot (1 table) to bitemporal SPO facts.

- ha_entity_snapshot → predicate ha_state. Content = state value (e.g. "on", "22.5"). Metadata = {attributes: <JSONB>, entity_id_ha: <HA entity string>}. entity_id = resolved HA device entity (or auto-created entity per HA device). valid_at = last_updated from HA. Property fact with supersession (each snapshot upserts = supersedes previous).

Key consideration: HA entities are not people — they're devices/sensors. May need entity_type expansion beyond person/organization/place/other, or use 'other' with metadata.entity_class = 'ha_device'.

Lower priority than health/relationship/finance — the snapshot table is small and read-only from the butler's perspective.

Acceptance criteria:
1. ha_entity_snapshot reads/writes facts instead of dedicated table
2. Entity resolution creates/reuses HA device entities in shared.entities
3. Supersession correctly replaces previous state on each snapshot cycle
4. Tests updated and passing
title: Migrate home butler ha_entity_snapshot to SPO facts

description:
After migrating CRUD tables to facts, aggregation tools (nutrition_summary, spending_summary, trend_report) will query facts.metadata JSONB for numeric values. Add indexes to support these patterns:

1. GIN index on facts.metadata for general JSONB containment queries (@>, ?, ?|, ?&)
2. Partial B-tree indexes on commonly extracted metadata fields for high-volume predicates:
   - (predicate, valid_at) WHERE predicate LIKE 'meal_%' AND validity = 'active' — meal aggregation
   - (predicate, valid_at) WHERE predicate LIKE 'transaction_%' AND validity = 'active' — spending aggregation
   - (predicate, valid_at) WHERE predicate LIKE 'measurement_%' AND validity = 'active' — health trends
3. Benchmark aggregation query performance before and after indexes with realistic data volumes.

Write as a migration (mem_008 or later, after the valid_at migration lands).

Acceptance criteria:
1. GIN index on facts.metadata exists
2. At least 3 partial predicate indexes for high-volume temporal predicates
3. Aggregation queries (nutrition_summary, spending_summary) execute in <100ms for 10k facts
title: Add GIN indexes on facts.metadata for JSONB aggregation queries

description:
Write idempotent data migration scripts that backfill existing rows from deprecated CRUD tables into the facts table. One script per phase:

1. Health: measurements, symptoms, medication_doses, medications, conditions, research → facts
2. Relationship: quick_facts, interactions, life_events, notes, gifts, loans, tasks, reminders, activity_feed → facts
3. Finance: transactions, accounts, subscriptions, bills → facts
4. Home: ha_entity_snapshot → facts

Each script must:
- Resolve entity_id for each row (owner entity for health/finance, contact entity for relationship)
- Generate embeddings via EmbeddingEngine for each fact
- Set valid_at from the source timestamp column (eaten_at, measured_at, occurred_at, posted_at, etc.)
- Set appropriate permanence (stable for chronic/long-term, standard for current state, volatile for acute)
- Skip rows that already have a corresponding active fact (idempotent)
- Log progress and errors
- Be runnable as a management command (e.g., python -m butlers.scripts.backfill_facts --phase health)

Acceptance criteria:
1. All 4 phase scripts exist and are idempotent
2. Entity resolution is correct (no generic 'user' subjects)
3. Embeddings are generated for all backfilled facts
4. Scripts can be re-run safely without duplicating data
title: Write data backfill scripts for CRUD-to-facts migration

description:
Add aliases support to predicate_registry for deterministic synonym resolution at write time.

**Schema:**
- Add aliases TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}' column to predicate_registry
- Include aliases in search_vector trigger: setweight(to_tsvector('english', array_to_string(aliases, ' ')), 'B')
- Create GIN index on aliases for containment queries

**Write-path resolution (in store_fact or writing.py):**
- Before registry lookup by predicate name, check if the (normalized) predicate matches any alias: SELECT name FROM predicate_registry WHERE $1 = ANY(aliases)
- If alias match found, silently rewrite predicate to the canonical name
- This is deterministic — no fuzzy matching needed for known synonyms

**Seed aliases for existing predicates:**
- parent_of → ['father_of', 'mother_of', 'dad_of', 'child_relationship']
- knows → ['friend_of', 'acquaintance_of', 'connected_to']
- works_at → ['employed_by', 'employed_at', 'job_at']
- sibling_of → ['brother_of', 'sister_of']
- lives_in → ['resides_in', 'located_in', 'based_in']

**Return alias resolution info in response:**
- When an alias is resolved, include 'resolved_from' in the store_fact response so the LLM learns the canonical form

Spec: openspec/changes/predicate-registry-enforcement/ (new requirement needed)
Depends on bu-2kk9 (search_vector trigger must incorporate aliases)
title: Predicate aliases for deterministic resolution

description:
Implement a self-healing mechanism for the butler ecosystem. When a butler encounters
an error, it reports it via an MCP tool (primary path) or the spawner catches hard
crashes (fallback path). The system fingerprints the error, deduplicates against
active investigations, and spawns a healing agent in an isolated git worktree to
investigate and propose a fix via PR — never merging into main by itself.

## Architecture

- **Primary path**: Self-healing MCP module (report_error + get_healing_status tools)
  wired into every butler. Butler agents call report_error with structured error
  context and diagnostic reasoning.
- **Secondary path**: Spawner fallback for hard crashes where the agent couldn't
  self-report.
- **Shared skill**: roster/shared/skills/self-healing/ teaches all butlers the protocol.
- **Dedicated model tier**: self_healing complexity tier in Model Catalog for cost isolation.
- Both paths converge on the same dispatch engine with 10-gate check sequence
  and atomic deduplication via partial unique index.

## OpenSpec References (source of truth)
- Proposal: openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/proposal.md
- Design: openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/design.md (14 decisions)
- Tasks: openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/tasks.md (12 groups, 55 tasks)
- Specs (11 files):
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/error-fingerprinting/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/self-healing-dispatch/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/self-healing-module/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/self-healing-skill/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/healing-worktree/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/healing-anonymizer/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/healing-session-tracking/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/healing-model-tier/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/core-spawner/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/core-sessions/spec.md
  - openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/model-catalog/spec.md

## Key Safety Constraints
- PRs only — never auto-merge into main
- Anonymizer is a hard gate — no PR without passing PII validation
- No recursive healing — trigger_source=healing sessions never re-enter dispatch
- Rate limits: per-fingerprint cooldown, concurrency cap, circuit breaker

description: When a message arrives from Telegram and is routed to a butler, the LLM runtime calls memory_entity_resolve(name=Owner, tenant_id=owner) instead of using request_context.source_sender_entity_id. This causes: (1) wrong tenant_id creating entities invisible to the dashboard, (2) fragile name-string matching instead of using the pre-resolved entity_id. The identity pipeline already resolves chat_id to entity_id and injects it into request_context, but the LLM ignores it. Root causes: stale spec (tenant_id default), no structured entity_id handoff to memory tools, and missing documentation of the full flow.
title: Ensure sender entity_id flows reliably from ingestion to memory storage

description:
Update three OpenSpec documents to close documentation gaps in the sender entity_id flow:

## 1. module-memory/spec.md — Fix stale tenant_id default + add entity_id handoff requirement

**Fix line 676**: Change entity_resolve tenant_id default from 'default' to 'shared'. This
aligns with entity-identity spec (owner entity has tenant_id='shared') and the code fix
already applied in this branch.

**Fix line 675**: Change entity_create tenant_id from 'required' to 'default shared'. All
entity MCP tools now default to tenant_id='shared'.

**Add new requirement**: 'Entity anchoring via request_context'. When a butler runtime
receives a routed message with request_context.source_sender_entity_id set, memory tools
SHOULD use that entity_id directly for fact storage rather than calling entity_resolve.
Document the expected flow:
  1. request_context.source_sender_entity_id is pre-resolved by Switchboard identity pipeline
  2. Butler runtime extracts entity_id from request_context (not from text preamble parsing)
  3. memory_store_fact is called with the extracted entity_id
  4. entity_resolve is only needed for third-party entities mentioned in the message content

## 2. switchboard-identity/spec.md — Strengthen the structured data requirement

The spec already says butlers MUST use entity_id for fact anchoring (line 42-43), but it only
describes the text preamble format. Add a scenario that explicitly requires
source_sender_entity_id to be present in request_context (it already is in code — this just
documents existing behavior in the spec):

- WHEN a message is routed via route.v1 envelope
- THEN request_context MUST contain source_sender_entity_id (when sender is resolved)
- AND request_context MUST contain source_sender_contact_id (when sender is resolved)

## 3. entity-identity/spec.md — Add tenant_id convention requirement

Add a requirement that all MCP entity tools MUST default tenant_id to 'shared'. The shared
schema is the single source of truth for identity entities. Per-butler tenant isolation is
a target-state requirement (module-memory spec line 1082-1098), not current behavior. Until
tenant isolation is fully implemented, 'shared' is the only valid tenant_id for identity
entities.

## Acceptance criteria

1. module-memory spec line 675-676 updated to reflect tenant_id='shared' defaults
2. module-memory spec has new requirement for entity_id handoff via request_context
3. switchboard-identity spec has scenario for structured entity_id in request_context
4. entity-identity spec has requirement that MCP entity tools default tenant_id='shared'
5. All spec changes follow existing ADDED Requirements / Scenario format
title: Update OpenSpecs: document entity_id flow from ingestion to memory storage

close_reason: Already implemented in 7ef16432: memory_store_fact auto-falls-back to source_entity_id from routing context when entity_id param is not explicitly provided (lines 290-300 of __init__.py)
description: The identity pipeline already resolves sender chat_id to entity_id and injects source_sender_entity_id into request_context. But downstream butler LLMs must parse a text preamble to extract it, which they frequently fail at (e.g. calling entity_resolve with name=Owner instead). Fix: make the butler daemon automatically extract source_sender_entity_id from the route.v1 request_context and pass it to memory_store_fact as the default entity_id. Approach: (A) Add a butler-level ContextVar or tool-scoped default that memory tools read when entity_id is not explicitly provided. (B) Alternatively, inject the resolved entity_id into the LLM system prompt in a structured, parseable format alongside the text preamble. (C) Or add a memory_store_fact_for_sender convenience tool that auto-resolves entity_id from request_context. Key files: src/butlers/daemon.py (route_execute, _routing_ctx_var), src/butlers/modules/memory/__init__.py (memory_store_fact tool), src/butlers/modules/pipeline.py (_set_routing_context). The chosen approach must work for both owner messages and third-party sender messages.
title: Inject sender entity_id into memory tool calls via request_context

description: The dashboard entity detail page at /api/memory/entities/{id} and entity list at /api/memory/entities both filter by tenant_id IN ('default', 'shared'). This means entities created with any other tenant_id (e.g. 'owner' due to LLM hallucination, or future tenant values) are invisible. With the code fix defaulting all entity tools to tenant_id='shared', new entities will be visible, but historical entities with wrong tenant_ids remain orphaned. Fix: (1) The dashboard queries should either remove the tenant_id filter entirely (since shared.entities is already access-controlled by schema grants) or include all known tenants. (2) Write a data migration to update any existing entities with tenant_id='owner' to tenant_id='shared'. Key files: src/butlers/api/routers/memory.py (lines 672, 790, 908, 1062).
title: Fix dashboard entity queries to include all valid tenant_ids
