Add a lightweight predicate_registry table to guide consistent predicate usage across fact extraction. Schema: name TEXT PRIMARY KEY, expected_subject_type TEXT, expected_object_type TEXT (NULL for property-facts), is_edge BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, description TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Not for enforcement - for prompt injection so LLM extractors prefer known predicates. Seed with existing predicates from fact-extraction SKILL.md taxonomy plus new edge predicates (knows, works_at, lives_with, manages, parent_of, sibling_of). Add MCP tool predicate_list() returning registry contents. Memory module migration.

design:
# Spec-to-Code Coverage Audit — Knowledge Graph Transition (bu-xd2)

## Updated Audit (2026-03-06, session 2)

All 5 gaps from session 1 have been addressed:

### GAP 1: Prompt/skill updates — FIXED by bu-xd2.5
consolidate/SKILL.md and fact-extraction/SKILL.md now include object_entity_id,
edge-fact guidelines, Step 5b, edge predicate taxonomy, and examples.

### GAP 2: entity_neighbors result schema — FIXED in this branch
Added direction, content, fact_id to CTE SELECT and result mapping.

### GAP 3: entity_neighbors default direction — FIXED in this branch
Changed default from 'outgoing' to 'both' per spec.

### GAP 4: entity_neighbors entity existence validation — FIXED in this branch
Added fetchval check before traversal query; raises ValueError if not found.

### GAP 5: store_fact self-referencing edge rejection — FIXED in this branch
Added entity_id == object_entity_id check before DB validation; raises ValueError.

## Full Coverage Checklist (all green)

| # | Requirement | Bead | Status |
|---|------------|------|--------|
| 1 | object_entity_id column on facts | bu-xd2.2 | COVERED |
| 2 | Property-fact uniqueness index | bu-xd2.2 | COVERED |

| 4 | store_fact accepts object_entity_id | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |

| 6 | store_fact requires entity_id when object_entity_id set | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |
| 7 | store_fact rejects self-referencing edges | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 8 | Edge-fact supersession keyed correctly | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |
| 9 | entity_neighbors tool with recursive CTE | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 10 | entity_neighbors registered as MCP tool | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 11 | entity_neighbors outgoing/incoming/both | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 12 | entity_neighbors default direction 'both' | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 13 | entity_neighbors predicate_filter | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 14 | entity_neighbors max_depth clamping | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 15 | entity_neighbors cycle detection | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 16 | entity_neighbors validity='active' | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 17 | entity_neighbors result schema (direction, content, fact_id) | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 18 | entity_neighbors validates entity existence | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 19 | predicate_registry table + seed data | bu-xd2.6 | COVERED |
| 20 | predicate_list() MCP tool | bu-xd2.6 | COVERED |
| 21 | entity_merge preserves roles (union) | bu-xd2.1 | COVERED |
| 22 | OpenSpec specs updated | bu-xd2.1 | COVERED |
| 23 | Prompt/skill updates for edge-facts | bu-xd2.5 | COVERED |

All spec requirements are now covered. No remaining gaps.

description: SelfHealingModule._handle_get_healing_status calls list_attempts(pool, limit=5) which fetches the 5 most recent healing attempts globally across all butlers, then Python-filters by butler_name. In multi-butler deployments with >5 global attempts from other butlers, this returns empty for the querying butler even when recent attempts exist. Fix: add butler_name parameter to list_attempts() in core/healing/tracking.py and push the filter into SQL. Discovered during PR review of #682 (bu-y8zn).
title: fix: list_attempts in get_healing_status filters globally, not per-butler

description:
The user-facing surface of self-healing: MCP module with report_error and
get_healing_status tools, shared skill teaching butlers the protocol, and
spawner except-block fallback for hard crashes.

## Work Items (tasks.md §8, §9, §10)
### Module (§8)
1. src/butlers/modules/self_healing/__init__.py — SelfHealingModule(Module) with
   name self_healing, config schema, no dependencies
2. register_tools() — report_error and get_healing_status MCP tools
3. report_error handler: structured error context→compute_fingerprint_from_report()
   →dispatch→return {accepted, fingerprint, reason}
4. get_healing_status handler: query by fingerprint or list recent for butler
5. tool_metadata() — mark error_message, traceback, context as sensitive
6. on_startup() — recover_stale_attempts() + reap_stale_worktrees()
7. on_shutdown() — cancel watchdog tasks
8. Tests for registration, handlers, response shapes, sensitivity

### Skill (§9)
9. roster/shared/skills/self-healing/SKILL.md — protocol for when/how to call
   report_error, data safety rules, response handling, examples
10. Update roster/shared/BUTLER_SKILLS.md to reference self-healing skill

### Spawner Fallback (§10)
11. asyncio.create_task(dispatch_healing(...)) in spawner except block — capture
    sys.exc_info() before cleanup, guard with trigger_source != "healing" AND
    self-healing module loaded
12. Per-butler semaphore bypass for trigger_source="healing" sessions
13. Empty mcp_servers dict for healing sessions; include GH_TOKEN in env
14. Integration test: hard crash→fallback fires→fingerprint→attempt created

## Covers
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/self-healing-module/spec.md — ALL requirements:
  → Module Identity, report_error MCP Tool, get_healing_status MCP Tool,
    Tool Sensitivity Metadata, Module Startup/Shutdown, Module Delegates to Core,
    Healing Agent Spawning from Module
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/self-healing-skill/spec.md — ALL requirements:
  → Skill Location/Discovery, Error Reporting Protocol, Handling the Response,
    Status Querying Protocol, Data Safety Instructions, Skill Content Structure
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/core-spawner/spec.md — ALL requirements:
  → Spawner Session Lifecycle (fallback dispatch), Trigger Source Tracking (healing),
    Healing Session Semaphore Bypass, Healing Session MCP Restriction,
    Healing Configuration in butler.toml, Dispatcher Startup on Daemon Boot
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/design.md §3 (Module), §4 (Shared skill)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. SelfHealingModule loads via module registry when [modules.self_healing] present
2. report_error MCP tool accepts structured context and returns {accepted, fingerprint, reason}
3. get_healing_status returns recent attempts or by-fingerprint lookup
4. Sensitivity metadata marks error_message, traceback, context as sensitive
5. on_startup() runs recovery + reaping before tools accept calls
6. SKILL.md teaches protocol: when/how to report, data safety, response handling
7. BUTLER_SKILLS.md references self-healing skill
8. Spawner fallback fires only for hard crashes when module is loaded
9. Healing sessions bypass per-butler semaphore, get empty MCP config + GH_TOKEN
10. Novelty gate deduplicates when both module and fallback fire for same error
11. Verify implementation matches scenarios in self-healing-module/spec.md,
    self-healing-skill/spec.md, and core-spawner/spec.md
title: Add self-healing MCP module, shared skill, and spawner fallback

description:
Foundation layer: database schema, enum extensions, and config that all other
self-healing beads depend on.

## Work Items (tasks.md §1, §2)
1. Alembic migration: extend complexity_tier CHECK constraint on shared.model_catalog
   and shared.butler_model_overrides to include self_healing
2. Alembic migration: create shared.healing_attempts table with indexes on fingerprint
   and status, plus partial unique index ON (fingerprint) WHERE status IN
   ('investigating', 'pr_open') for atomic novelty gate
3. Alembic migration: add healing_fingerprint TEXT (nullable) to each butler's sessions table
4. Add healing-sonnet seed entry to model_catalog_defaults.toml (tier self_healing,
   runtime claude, model claude-sonnet-4-6, priority 10)
5. Add SELF_HEALING = "self_healing" to Complexity enum in src/butlers/core/model_routing.py
6. Update valid tier list in src/butlers/api/routers/model_settings.py to include self_healing
7. Update TRIGGER_SOURCES frozenset in src/butlers/core/sessions.py to include "healing"

## Covers
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/healing-model-tier/spec.md
  → Req: Self-Healing Complexity Tier, Seed Data, API Validation Update
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/healing-session-tracking/spec.md
  → Req: Healing Attempts Table (schema, indexes, partial unique)
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/model-catalog/spec.md
  → Modified Req: Model Catalog Schema (Valid complexity tiers), Seed Data Migration
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/specs/core-sessions/spec.md
  → Modified Req: Trigger Source Tracking (add "healing"), Minimum Persisted Session Fields (add healing_fingerprint)
- openspec/changes/butler-self-healing/design.md §9 (Self-healing model tier), §11 (Database schema)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. make check passes with new migrations applied
2. Complexity enum includes SELF_HEALING; resolve_model(any_butler, "self_healing") can resolve healing-sonnet
3. TRIGGER_SOURCES accepts "healing" without ValueError
4. shared.healing_attempts table exists with all columns, indexes, and partial unique index
5. Per-butler sessions tables have healing_fingerprint column (nullable)
6. Dashboard model settings API accepts self_healing as valid tier
7. Verify implementation matches behavior defined in specs cited above
title: Add self-healing DB migrations, model tier, and trigger source

description:
## What

Create `tests/contracts/` directory and populate it with ~200 focused tests that
validate the 15 architectural invariants from heart-and-soul doctrine plus key
wire contracts from RFCs 0010-0012. These are the safety net that MUST exist
before any test deletion begins.

## Why

Currently, contract-level tests are buried among 13,675 implementation tests.
Extracting them gives us confidence to delete the rest — if these pass, the
doctrine holds.

## Scope

Write or extract tests for each invariant. Reference the specific RFC/principle
each test validates via docstring. Tag all with @pytest.mark.contract.

### Invariants to cover (~10-15 tests each):

1. **Schema isolation** — Butler can't query another butler's schema (RFC 0006)
2. **MCP-only inter-butler** — No cross-butler imports, no direct DB calls
3. **Daemon determinism** — 17-phase startup order, fatal vs non-fatal failure propagation (RFC 0001)
4. **Tool surface isolation** — Ephemeral MCP config scoping, session sandboxing (RFC 0002)
5. **Module composition** — Topo sort, cycle detection, cascade failure (RFC 0002)
6. **Module boundaries** — Modules MUST NOT modify core infrastructure (RFC 0002)
7. **Credential tier resolution** — Tier 0→1→2 precedence, env fallback, no plaintext leakage (RFC 0006)
8. **Approval gates** — Sensitive ops intercepted, bypass attempts rejected, timeout = denial (RFC 0002, security.md)
9. **Graceful shutdown** — Drain, reverse-order on_shutdown (RFC 0001)
10. **Session lifecycle** — request_id UUIDv7 propagation, tool call capture, token tracking (RFC 0001)
11. **Identity resolution** — 3-table JOIN, owner bootstrap, temp entity handling, preamble format (RFC 0004)
12. **Context bus** — Signal TTL, write permissions matrix, supersession semantics (RFC 0009)
13. **Routing pipeline** — Dedup (3 tiers), thread affinity, triage rules, LLM fallback, priority queuing (RFC 0003)
14. **Connector-as-transport** — Connectors normalize to ingest.v1 only; no routing, classification, or domain logic (vision.md Rule 7)
15. **Staffer routing exclusion** — Staffers excluded from user-message routing candidates (RFC 0003, vision.md Rule 3)

### Additional wire contracts to cover (~5 tests each):

16. **Cross-butler briefing exception** — Read-only view exists, 5 guardrails enforced, reuse criteria (RFC 0010)
17. **Insight delivery anti-spam** — Single entry point, global budget cap, cooldown enforcement, adaptive ratchet (RFC 0011)
18. **Finance soft-delete** — No hard DELETE on finance.transactions, tiered dedup indexes exist (RFC 0012)

## TDD Preservation

Before other beads delete tests, this bead ensures the architectural safety net
exists. The condensation principle: "delete implementation tests only when
contract tests already guard the same invariant."

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `tests/contracts/` directory exists with per-invariant test files (≥15 files)
2. Each test has a docstring citing its RFC or heart-and-soul principle
3. All tests pass: `pytest tests/contracts/ -q`
4. Tests are tagged with `@pytest.mark.contract` marker
5. pytest marker is registered in pyproject.toml/conftest
6. ≥200 total contract tests covering all 15 invariants + 3 wire contract groups
7. No tests depend on mocks for the invariant being validated (use real DB where possible)
title: Extract architectural contract tests into tests/contracts/

description:
The previous shared→public migration (core_002 identity tables, relationship contacts) left the shared schema alive because core_042 re-created it for shared.user_context. This epic eliminates the shared schema entirely — no table, index, search_path entry, or runtime reference should remain.

Inventory of remaining shared references:
- shared.user_context table — created by core_042, queried by context_bus.py (4 SQL statements), referenced in spawner.py error messages
- alembic/env.py — CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS shared and SET search_path TO own, shared, public on every migration run
- Scripts — backfill_transitory_entities.py, migrate_fact_subjects.py, migrate_blobs_to_s3.py all set search_path with shared
- Tests — ~20 test files create shared schema or reference shared.* tables
- Index/constraint names — core_002_identity.py left behind chk_shared_entities_*, idx_shared_entities_*, idx_shared_contact_info_* etc. on public tables

Non-goals: shared_db_name/shared_pool in credential_store.py and deps.py refer to the credential store topology (shared DB pool concept), not the PostgreSQL schema — those stay.
title: Eliminate shared schema — migrate all tables to public

close_reason: PR #959 merged after PR review — core_046 migration moves shared.user_context to public schema
description:
Write a new Alembic core migration (core_043) that moves shared.user_context into the public schema and drops the shared schema.

Migration steps:
1. ALTER TABLE shared.user_context SET SCHEMA public (moves table + indexes + constraints in one op)
2. Verify shared schema is empty: SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'shared'
3. DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS shared CASCADE

Runtime code updates (same bead — tightly coupled):
- src/butlers/context_bus.py: Change all 4 SQL references from shared.user_context to public.user_context (lines 184, 228, 318, 363). Also update module docstring (line 3) and ContextEntry docstring (line 39).
- src/butlers/core/spawner.py: Update error message at line 505 from "shared.user_context" to "public.user_context"

Design notes:
- Use ALTER TABLE SET SCHEMA rather than CREATE+INSERT+DROP to preserve OIDs, indexes, constraints, and avoid data copy.
- The partial index idx_user_context_active_signals will move automatically with ALTER TABLE SET SCHEMA.
- Use public.user_context (explicit schema) rather than unqualified, for clarity and to avoid search_path ambiguity.

Acceptance criteria:
1. New core_043 migration exists and passes alembic upgrade/downgrade
2. shared schema no longer exists after migration runs
3. context_bus.py queries use public.user_context
4. spawner.py error messages reference public.user_context
5. Existing context_bus tests pass with the new table location
title: Migrate shared.user_context to public schema

description:

- tests/modules/memory/test_memory_migration_integration.py (lines 159, 192-194): Update search_path documentation/assertions

Roster test files that create shared schema for identity tables:

Acceptance criteria:

4. No test creates a shared schema as a side effect

description:
Write a new Alembic core migration to rename indexes and constraints that still carry the shared_ prefix from the original identity table creation (core_002_identity.py). These objects live in the public schema but have misleading names.

Objects to rename (all on public schema tables):
- chk_shared_entities_entity_type → chk_entities_entity_type (on public.entities)
- idx_shared_entities_entity_type → idx_entities_entity_type
- idx_shared_entities_name → idx_entities_name
- idx_shared_entities_name_trgm → idx_entities_name_trgm
- idx_shared_entities_aliases → idx_entities_aliases
- idx_shared_entities_aliases_trgm → idx_entities_aliases_trgm
- idx_shared_entities_updated_at → idx_entities_updated_at
- Any idx_shared_contact_info_* → idx_contact_info_*
- Any idx_shared_entity_info_* → idx_entity_info_*

Use ALTER INDEX ... RENAME TO and ALTER TABLE ... RENAME CONSTRAINT for the rename operations.

Note: Also update core_002_identity.py downgrade path if it references these names (the downgrade should drop by the new names).

Acceptance criteria:
1. New migration renames all shared_* prefixed indexes/constraints to non-shared equivalents
2. Migration is reversible (downgrade restores old names)
3. No index or constraint in the public schema contains "shared" in its name after migration
4. Existing queries are unaffected (PostgreSQL index renames are metadata-only)
title: Rename legacy shared_* index and constraint names on public tables

close_reason: Gen-1 reconciliation complete: identified 3 gaps (bu-3ozko, bu-yke8s, bu-szh61), all fixed and merged. Gen-2 follow-up confirmed full coverage.
description:
Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (see epic bu-zszt description) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
3. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement to its implementing bead.
4. Run a comprehensive grep for any remaining shared schema references: grep -rn "shared\." src/ alembic/ scripts/ tests/ roster/ — filter out false positives (English prose, credential_store shared_pool, etc.)
5. Verify the shared schema does not exist in any test database fixtures.
6. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
7. If gap beads were created in step 6, create a follow-up reconciliation bead (gen-2) that depends on ALL the new gap beads.
8. Keep this reconciliation bead open (or blocked) until all newly created gap beads are closed.
9. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Every requirement in the epic spec has a corresponding implementation bead
2. Any gaps found result in new child beads created under the same epic
3. If gaps were found, a follow-up reconciliation bead (gen-2) was created
4. Reconciliation summary is recorded in the close reason
5. grep -rn for shared as a DB schema across entire codebase returns zero hits (excluding credential_store shared_pool and English prose)
title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage for shared schema elimination (gen-1)

description:
Write the `transaction-csv-extraction` SKILL.md that guides the finance butler's LLM runtime through adaptive CSV import via script generation.

## What
Create `roster/finance/.agents/skills/transaction-csv-extraction/SKILL.md` with:
- Valid frontmatter (name, description, version)
- **CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS section (top of skill, before workflow)**: explicit MUST NOT for full-file context loading and per-row tool calls, with stated reasons (token budget). Imperative voice (MUST NOT / NEVER), not advisory.
- **Sampling phase**: read header + first 10 rows, infer column mapping, date format, delimiter, amount convention (single column vs split debit/credit)
- **Script generation phase**: stdlib-only Python (`csv`, `json`, `urllib.request`, `datetime`, `hashlib`, `sys`), batch POST of 100 to `/api/finance/transactions/bulk`, BOM handling, JSON summary to stdout, `--api-url` CLI arg, `BUTLERS_API_URL` env var, pre-flight HEAD request
- **Execution + self-correction**: run script, read stderr on failure, fix and re-run (max 3 attempts)
- **Result reporting**: parse JSON summary, report totals, warn on >20% skip rate
- **Worked examples**: at least 2 end-to-end examples (simple CSV, and one with split debit/credit + non-standard dates)

## Covers
- `openspec/changes/transaction-csv-extraction/specs/csv-extraction-skill/spec.md` — all requirements
- `openspec/changes/transaction-csv-extraction/design.md` — decisions 1, 4, 5

## Key Design Constraint
The skill prompt is the ONLY mechanism controlling the LLM's runtime behavior. Without emphatic, unambiguous prompting, the LLM WILL default to reading the whole file and calling `record_transaction` per row — a token budget catastrophe. The constraints must be impossible to misinterpret.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. SKILL.md has valid frontmatter with name, description, version
2. CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS section appears before the workflow steps
3. Constraints use imperative form (MUST NOT, NEVER) and state the reason (token budget)
4. Workflow covers all 4 phases: sample → generate script → execute → report
5. Script generation instructions specify stdlib-only, batch size 100, JSON summary output, --api-url override, BUTLERS_API_URL env var, pre-flight connectivity check
6. Self-correction loop is capped at 3 attempts
7. High skip rate (>20%) triggers user warning about potential false dedup
8. At least 2 worked examples with different CSV formats
9. Verify implementation matches behavior defined in csv-extraction-skill/spec.md
